description: During the initial phase of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, the Japanese government pushed for the emergency approval of Avigan (favipiravir). The debate centered on the pressure to secure a domestic antiviral solution versus the lack of robust, peer-reviewed clinical evidence of its efficacy.

key_events.description: Prime Minister Abe publicly promotes Avigan as a potential game-changer.
Clinical studies show negligible impact on recovery time in severe cases.
Japanese medical associations issue warnings against off-label use.
Government officially halts the fast-track approval process.

calibration_notes: Highlights the tension between nationalistic scientific ambition and evidence-based global health standards.
